claims.claimText: Surgical approaches form a reusable operative exposure backbone that links positioning, landmarks, intervals, protection, extensile options, bailout, and closure across specialties.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Approach selection is driven by the required target, soft-tissue condition, prior incisions, imaging needs, and defined extensile or bailout pathways—not implant brand preference.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Positioning must permit the planned exposure, fluoroscopy, anesthesia access, and a safe extensile or bailout option before prep and drape.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: C-arm trajectory, sterile entry path, and monitor position should be verified before incision so imaging is not obstructed by drapes or equipment.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Surface landmarks define incision placement and must be confirmed after positioning because landmarks shift with limb rotation and traction.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Skin incision planning accounts for prior scars, extensile continuity, soft-tissue envelopes, and anticipated implant or reduction needs.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Internervous planes reduce denervation risk and should be recorded as reusable anatomy objects independent of any single named approach.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Intermuscular planes define standard intervals and must be distinguished from improvised muscle-splitting paths.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Deep intervals complete exposure after the superficial plane and often determine whether the target is safely reached.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Every named approach has exposure limits; exceeding them without a defined extensile pathway increases neurovascular and soft-tissue risk.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Structures requiring protection are identified before incision and rechecked during retraction, instrumentation, and closure.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Neurovascular structures at risk are approach-defining safety objects; loss of identification is a stop signal.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Extensile options are planned independently of the standard exposure so extension follows a defined pathway rather than ad hoc dissection.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Bailout strategies—alternate windows, staged exposure, or change of approach—are selected before incision when risk is high.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Closure restores released layers without entrapping protected structures and accounts for swelling, drains, and postoperative restrictions.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Postoperative approach considerations include wound surveillance, motion or weight-bearing limits linked to the exposure, and approach-specific complication watch-outs.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Revision exposure reuses prior incisions when safe, but may require alternate windows when soft-tissue or neurovascular risk is elevated.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: If exposure is inadequate, escalate along defined extensile or bailout pathways rather than forcing the original window.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Safe zones constrain implant and instrument trajectories relative to known neurovascular anatomy and should be treated as first-class operative objects.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Approach-specific complications are linked to the exposure pathway and inform selection, counseling, and intraoperative protection priorities.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: The deltopectoral approach uses the deltopectoral interval and prioritizes axillary nerve and cephalic vein protection.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: The volar Henry approach follows the Henry interval with continuous protection of the radial artery and median nerve territories.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Kocher-Langenbeck exposure prioritizes sciatic nerve protection and medial femoral circumflex preservation for femoral head blood supply.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Direct anterior hip exposure uses the Smith-Petersen interval and requires attention to the lateral femoral cutaneous nerve and proximal femoral anatomy.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: The medial parapatellar approach provides extensile knee exposure while protecting the extensor mechanism and planning for staged extension if needed.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Posterior spine approaches require continuous orientation to the cord and nerve roots; unintended durotomy or neural injury is a stop-and-repair event.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Lateral lumbar approaches traverse the psoas corridor and require neuromonitoring attention to plexus risk.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

claims.claimText: Positioning objects are shared; do not mint approach-specific duplicate positioning identities for the same standard position.

metadata.sourceNote: Surgical Approaches manufacture; approach-selection claims require human review

decisionPoints.trigger: Procedure requires operative exposure and multiple approaches are plausible
decisionPoints.action: Select the approach whose safe window reaches the target while accounting for soft tissue, prior incisions, imaging, and extensile/bailout needs

decisionPoints.trigger: Positioning does not permit safe imaging, anesthesia access, or bailout exposure
decisionPoints.action: Reposition and verify all required access before preparation and incision

decisionPoints.trigger: Required fluoroscopic views cannot be obtained after positioning
decisionPoints.action: Adjust table, C-arm, or patient position before prep rather than accepting inadequate imaging

decisionPoints.trigger: Surface landmarks are distorted by swelling, deformity, or obesity
decisionPoints.action: Confirm landmarks with imaging and extensile incision planning before committing the skin cut

decisionPoints.trigger: A protected neurovascular structure cannot be identified or safely mobilized
decisionPoints.action: Stop blind progression, restore landmarks, extend along a defined pathway, or change approach

decisionPoints.trigger: The planned internervous or intermuscular interval is not clearly identified
decisionPoints.action: Re-establish landmarks and interval identity before deep dissection

decisionPoints.trigger: Target anatomy cannot be reached within the approach exposure limits
decisionPoints.action: Invoke the predefined extensile option or bailout strategy rather than improvising

decisionPoints.trigger: Additional exposure is required beyond the standard window
decisionPoints.action: Extend only along the defined extensile pathway while rechecking protected structures

decisionPoints.trigger: The planned approach is unsafe or inadequate despite defined extension
decisionPoints.action: Execute the preplanned bailout: alternate window, staged exposure, or change of approach

decisionPoints.trigger: Prior incisions, implants, or scarring alter standard intervals
decisionPoints.action: Choose re-entry versus alternate window with attending-level plan for neurovascular protection

decisionPoints.trigger: Swelling, tissue viability, or tension makes layered primary closure unsafe
decisionPoints.action: Use defined bailout soft-tissue strategy and postoperative protection plan

decisionPoints.trigger: Anterior shoulder or proximal humerus exposure is required
decisionPoints.action: Use deltopectoral interval with axillary nerve protection and planned extensile option

decisionPoints.trigger: Posterior acetabular column or wall exposure is required
decisionPoints.action: Use KL pathway with sciatic protection and MFCA-aware dissection; escalate if visualization is inadequate

decisionPoints.trigger: Anterior hip arthroplasty or anterior hip exposure is planned
decisionPoints.action: Confirm table/positioning strategy and LFCN/femoral nerve protection before incision

decisionPoints.trigger: Neural elements are not clearly visualized or protected during lumbar exposure
decisionPoints.action: Stop, reorient, improve exposure, and protect cord/roots before continuing instrumentation

decisionPoints.trigger: Popliteal neurovascular structures are not controlled during posterior knee exposure
decisionPoints.action: Stop deep progression until popliteal vessels and nerves are identified and protected
